title: gladiolus segetum subsp. ludovicae
intro: Gladiolus segetum subsp. ludovicae, also known as the wild gladiolus or gladiolus of the fields, is a type of plant that belongs to the iris family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gladiolus segetum subsp. ludovicae include its ability to relieve congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
